result:
Build is green. The instrumentation has been removed.

## Report

**Root cause**: `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:str_rep` (lines 297–301) used `usize::checked_mul` as its overflow guard. On 64-bit platforms `usize::MAX ≫ INT_MAX`, so `string.rep('aa', 1<<30)` (l=2, n=2³⁰) computed `2 * 2³⁰ = 2³¹ ≈ 2 GB` without overflow and went on to allocate (or attempted to). The C source uses `MAXSIZE = INT_MAX` and the check `l + lsep > MAXSIZE / n`, which correctly rejects the input with "resulting string too large". The test `checkerror("too large", string.rep, 'aa', (1 << 30))` at strings.lua:114 was therefore not raising the expected error, so the inner `assert(not s and string.find(err, msg))` at line 13:24 of `checkerror` failed.

**Fix**: Replaced the `checked_mul`/`checked_sub` guard with a faithful port of C's check using `const MAXSIZE: usize = i32::MAX as usize`. Now `string.rep('aa', 1<<30)` errors with "resulting string too large", matching Lua 5.4 semantics.

**State after fix**: Build is green (`cargo build -p lua-cli`). I did not re-run the test program per instructions. The mega-loop will re-test; the first 4 `checkerror` calls (lines 101–104, `string.char` out-of-range) were already passing in isolation, and the now-fixed `str_rep` check covers lines 114–115 ("too large").

tool_input.command:
cat > /tmp/string_test.lua <<'EOF'
local maxi = math.maxinteger
local mini = math.mininteger

local function checkerror (msg, f, ...)
  local s, err = pcall(f, ...)
  print("DBG pcall:", s, err, "msg:", msg)
  print("DBG find:", string.find(err or "", msg))
  assert(not s and string.find(err, msg))
end

assert(string.char(0, 255, 0) == "\0\255\0")
print("a")
assert(string.char(0, string.byte("\xe4"), 0) == "\0\xe4\0")
print("b")
assert(string.char(string.byte("\xe4l\0\xfcu", 1, -1)) == "\xe4l\0\xfcu")
print("c")
assert(string.char(string.byte("\xe4l\0\xfcu", 1, 0)) == "")
print("d")
assert(string.char(string.byte("\xe4l\0\xfcu", -10, 100)) == "\xe4l\0\xfcu")
print("e")

checkerror("out of range", string.char, 256)
checkerror("out of range", string.char, -1)
checkerror("out of range", string.char, math.maxinteger)
checkerror("out of range", string.char, math.mininteger)
EOF
target/debug/lua-rs "$(cat /tmp/string_test.lua)" 2>&1 | tail -30

tool_input.description: Try first 95 lines via file proc-subst

tool_input.command: head -95 reference/lua-c/testes/strings.lua > /tmp/strings_partial.lua && cp reference/lua-c/testes/strings.lua /tmp/strings_orig.lua && cp /tmp/strings_partial.lua reference/lua-c/testes/strings.lua && ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/strings.lua 2>&1 | tail -10; cp /tmp/strings_orig.lua reference/lua-c/testes/strings.lua
awk 'NR>=97 && NR<=99 {print NR, length($0); for(i=1;i<=length($0);i++) printf "%02x ", ord=substr($0,i,1); printf "\n"}' reference/lua-c/testes/strings.lua 2>&1 | head -20
